title: Jim
content:
Who ran away from his Nurse and was eaten by a Lion

title: Introduction: The Bad Child's Book Of Beasts
content:
I call you bad, my little child,
    Upon the title page,
    Because a manner rude and wild
    Is common at your age.

title: The Tiger
content:
The tiger, on the other hand,
    Is kittenish and mild,
    And makes a pretty playfellow
    For any little child.
    And mothers of large

title: Hildebrand
content:
Who was frightened by a Passing Motor, and was brought to Reason

    "Oh murder! What was that, Papa!"

title: The Night
content:
Most Holy Night, that still dost keep
    The keys of all the doors of sleep,
    To me when my tired eyelids close
    Give thou repose.

title: The Pacifist
content:
Pale Ebenezer thought it wrong to fight,
    But Roaring Bill (who killed him) thought it right.

title: The Vulture
content:
The Vulture eats between his meals,
    And that's the reason why
    He very, very, rarely feels
    As well as you and I.

    His eye is du

title: The Death And Last Confession Of Wandering Peter
content:
When Peter Wanderwide was young
    He wandered everywhere he would:
    All that he approved was sung,
    And most of what he saw was good.

title: On Vital Statistics
content:

title: Lord Finchley
content:
Lord Finchley tried to mend the Electric Light
    Himself. It struck him dead: And serve him right!
    It is the business of the wealthy man

title: The Dromedary
content:
The Dromedary is a cheerful bird:
    I cannot say the same about the Kurd.

title: On Torture: A Public Singer
content:
Torture will give a dozen pence or more
    To keep a drab from bawling at his door.
    The public taste is quite a different thing
    Tortur

title: The Evenlode
content:
I will not try to reach again,
    I will not set my sail alone,
    To moor a boat bereft of men
    At Yarnton's tiny docks of stone.

    B

title: The South Country
content:
When I am living in the Midlands
    That are sodden and unkind,
    I light my lamp in the evening:
    My work is left behind;

title: Godolphin Horne
content:
Who was cursed with the Sin of Pride, and Became a Boot-Black.

    Godolphin Horne was Nobly Born;
    He held the Human Race in Scorn,

title: On The Ladies Of Pixton
content:
Three Graces; and the mother were a Grace,
    But for profounder meaning in her face.

title: Time Cures All
content:
It was my shame, and now it is my boast,
    That I have loved you rather more than most.

title: Franklin Hyde
content:
Who caroused in the Dirt and was corrected by His Uncle.

    Carousing in the Dirt.

title: Kings Live In Palaces, And Pigs In Sties
content:
Kings live in Palaces, and Pigs in sties,
    And youth in Expectation. Youth is wise.

title: Tarantella
content:
Do you remember an Inn,
    Miranda?
    Do you remember an Inn?
    And the tedding and the bedding
    Of the straw for a bedding,

title: The Early Morning
content:
The moon on the one hand, the dawn on the other:
    The moon is my sister, the dawn is my brother.
    The moon on my left and the dawn on my r

title: The Whale
content:
The Whale that wanders round the Pole
    Is not a table fish.
    You cannot bake or boil him whole
    Nor serve him in a dish;

    But you

title: The Marmozet
content:
The species Man and Marmozet
    Are intimately linked;
    The Marmozet survives as yet,
    But Men are all extinct.

title: The Pelagian Drinking Song
content:
Pelagius lived at Kardanoel
    And taught a doctrine there
    How, whether you went to heaven or to hell
    It was your own affair.
    It

title: Rebecca
content:
Who Slammed Doors For Fun And Perished Miserably

    A trick that everyone abhors
    In little girls is slamming doors.

title: The Microbe
content:
The Microbe is so very small
    You cannot make him out at all,
    But many sanguine people hope
    To see him through a microscope.
    Hi

title: Henry King
content:
The Chief Defect of Henry King
    Was chewing little bits of String.
    At last he swallowed some which tied
    Itself in ugly Knots inside.

title: Ha'nacker Mill
content:
Sally is gone that was so kindly,
    Sally is gone from Ha'nacker Hill
    And the Briar grows ever since then so blindly;
    And ever since

title: Because My Faltering Feet
content:
Because my faltering feet may fail to dare
    The first descendant of the steps of Hell
    Give me the Word in time that triumphs there.

title: The World Is Full Of Double Beds
content:
The world is full of double beds
    And most delightful maidenheads,
    Which being so, there�s no excuse
    For sodomy of self-abuse.

title: The Frog
content:
Be kind and tender to the Frog,
    And do not call him names,
    As "Slimy skin," or "Polly-wog,"
    Or likewise "Ugly James,"
    Or "Gap-

title: The Big Baboon
content:
The Big Baboon is found upon
    The plains of Cariboo:
    He goes about with nothing on
    (A shocking thing to do).

    But if he dressed

title: The Elephant
content:
When people call this beast to mind,
    They marvel more and more
    At such a little tail behind,
    So large a trunk before.

title: September
content:
I, from a window where the Meuse is wide,
    Looked eastward out to the September night;
    The men that in the hopeless battle died
    Rose

title: Algernon
content:
Who played with a Loaded Gun, and, on missing his Sister was reprimanded by his Father.

    Was playing w

title: Heretics All
content:
Heretics all, whoever you may be,
    In Tarbes or Nimes, or over the sea,
    You never shall have good words from me.
    Caritas non conturb

title: October
content:
Look, how those steep woods on the mountain's face
    Burn, burn against the sunset; now the cold
    Invades our very noon: the year's grown o

title: Juliet
content:
How did the party go in Portman Square?
    I cannot tell you; Juliet was not there.

    And how did Lady Gaster's party go?
    Juliet was ne

title: Lines For A Christmas Card
content:
May all my enemies go to hell,
    Noel, Noel, Noel, Noel.

title: The Statue
content:
When we are dead, some Hunting-boy will pass
    And find a stone half-hidden in tall grass
    And grey with age: but having seen that stone

title: Lord Lundy
content:
Who was too Freely Moved to Tears, and thereby ruined his Political Career

    Lord Lundy from his earliest years
    Was far too freely moved

title: The Hippopotamus
content:
I shoot the Hippopotamus
    With bullets made of platinum,
    Because if I use leaden ones
    His hide is sure to flatten 'em.

title: Ballade To Our Lady Of Czestochowa
content:

    And very Regent of the untroubled sky,
    Whom in a dream St. Hilda did behold

title: The Lion
Heroic Poem In Praise Of Wine
content:
To exalt, enthrone, establish and defend,
    To welcome home mankind's mysterious friend
    Wine, true begetter of all arts that be;

title: Is There Any reward?
content:
Is there any reward?
    I'm beginning to doubt it.
    I am broken and bored,
    Is there any reward
    Reassure me, Good Lord,
    And in

title: Charles Augustus Fortescue
content:
The nicest child I ever knew
    Was Charles Augustus Fortescue.
    He never lost his cap, or tore
    His stockings or his pinafore:
    In

title: Introduction: More Beasts For Worse Children
content:
The parents of the learned child
    (His father and his mother)
    Were utterly aghast to note
    The facts he would at random quote
    On

title: The Birds
content:
When Jesus Christ was four years old
    The angels brought Him toys of gold,
    Which no man ever had bought or sold.

    And yet with these

title: On The Little God
content:
Of all the gods that gave me all their glories
    To-day there deigns to walk with me but one.
    I lead him by the hand and tell him stories.

title: George
content:
Who played with a Dangerous Toy, and suffered a Catastrophe of considerable Dimensions

    When George's Grandmamma was told
    That George ha

title: The Scorpion
content:
The Scorpion is as black as soot,
    He dearly loves to bite;
    He is a most unpleasant brute
    To find in bed at night.

title: The Telephone
content:
To-night in million-voiced London I
    Was lonely as the million-pointed sky
    Until your single voice. Ah! So the sun
    Peoples all heave

title: Drinking Song, On the Excellence of Burgundy Wine
content:
My jolly fat host with your face all a-grin,
    Come, open the door to us, let us come in.
    A score of stout fellows who think it no sin

title: Talking (And Singing) Of The Nordic Man
content:
I

    Behold, my child, the Nordic man,
    And be as like him, as you can;
    His legs are long, his mind is slow,
    His hair is lank and mad

title: A Trinity
content:
Of three in One and One in three
    My narrow mind would doubting be
    Till Beauty, Grace and Kindness met
    And all at once were Juliet.

title: The Catholic Sun
content:
Wherever the Catholic sun doth shine,
    There�s always laughter and good red wine.
    At least I�ve always found it so.
    Benedicamus Domi

title: Lines To A Don
content:
Remote and ineffectual Don
    That dared attack my Chesterton,
    With that poor weapon, half-impelled,
    Unlearnt, unsteady, hardly held,

title: he Scorpion
content:
The Scorpion is as black as soot,
    He dearly loves to bite;
    He is a most unpleasant brute
    To find in bed at night.

title: On Two Ministers Of State
content:
Lump says that Caliban's of gutter breed,
    And Caliban says Lump's a fool indeed,
    And Caliban and Lump and I are all agreed.
